journal_entry.rationale: Inventory loss due to natural disaster is recognized as an expense (loss). The inventory asset account is reduced by the carrying value of the destroyed inventory. Under IFRS, casualty losses are typically recorded as other expenses or losses. This is a non-taxable event (no HST/GST applies to inventory write-offs due to casualty).

output.reason: Inventory destroyed = asset decrease. The inventory (an asset) is written off due to the typhoon damage, so we credit inventory to reduce its balance.

credit_classifier.reason: Inventory destroyed = asset decrease. The inventory (an asset) is written off due to the typhoon damage, so we credit inventory to reduce its balance.

output.reason: Inventory destroyed by typhoon represents a casualty loss. The debit side records an expense increase (loss from casualty/disaster). The inventory asset will be credited (decreased) on the credit side.
